meaning: n.<美>[动](墨西哥与美国西部产的)叉角羚
examples: Herds of elk, deer, and pronghorn antelope roam the grasslands, where visitors can camp, hike, and bike.
When the American cheetahs disappeared, their influence on the evolution of pronghorn and presumably on other prey animals stopped.
Increasing development is leading toward a crisis for the pronghorn, threatening to choke off their passageway.
Round out your escape with a signature Pronghorn cocktail while you watch the sunset at Cascada Lounge.
Hunters of elk, mule deer, and pronghorn antelope are even more devoted--there can be a two-year waiting list during hunting season.
